steps.title: Identify the contrasting ideas
steps.content: Sentences 1-2 list Hammurabi's major achievements: conquering all of Mesopotamia and building Babylon into a powerful city. The sentence with the blank states that 'today' he is mainly remembered for one specific thing: the Code of Hammurabi.

steps.title: Determine the logical relationship
steps.content: Despite his many grand accomplishments, his lasting legacy is narrower than expected. The transition must signal contrast between his actual achievements and what he is now known for.

steps.title: Eliminate by rule violated
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'therefore,': signals cause-effect, but his being remembered for the Code is not a logical consequence of conquering Mesopotamia. ✗
  • Choice B — 'likewise,': signals similarity, but being remembered for a law code is not similar to military conquest. ✗
  • Choice C — 'however,': signals contrast between his many achievements and his single primary legacy. ✓
  • Choice D — 'for instance,': signals an example, but the Code is not an example of his conquests or city-building. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
